ucs.title: [LLM] Mofang spearphishing-attachment chain: Office app spawns loader from compressed/encrypted dropper
ucs.description: Hunts the Mofang (G0103) initial-access pattern where a spearphishing attachment (T1566.001) is opened by the victim (T1204.002), an Office host application drops a compressed/encrypted payload (T1027.013/T1027.015 — ShimRat loader family) and immediately spawns a script-host or LOLBin child. Mofang is documented (FOX-IT, 2016) using this exact Word/Excel→dropper→ShimRat sequence against Myanmar government and defense-industrial targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as child_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\Public\\*") AND Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.zip","*.rar","*.cab","*.7z","*.iso","*.img","*.dat","*.bin")) by host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")] | join type=inner [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Email.All_Email where (All_Email.file_name IN ("*.zip","*.rar","*.7z","*.iso","*.img","*.docx","*.doc","*.xls","*.xlsm","*.lnk") AND All_Email.action="delivered") by All_Email.recipient All_Email.src_user All_Email.subject All_Email.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | rename recipient as user] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime host user parent_process_name process_name cmdline file_name src_user subject child_hash
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let LoaderChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let ArchiveExt = dynamic([".zip",".rar",".cab",".7z",".iso",".img",".dat",".bin"]);
// 1. Spearphishing attachment delivered (T1566.001) — archive/Office attachment
let PhishMail = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailAttachmentInfo
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where FileType in~ ("ZIP","RAR","7Z","ISO","IMG","DOC","DOCX","XLS","XLSM","LNK")
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | project MailTime = Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress,
              Subject, AttachmentName = FileName, AttachmentSHA256 = SHA256;
// 2. Office app drops a compressed/encrypted secondary payload (T1027.013/T1027.015)
let Drops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | where tolower(FileName) endswith_any (ArchiveExt) or FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(dat|bin|tmp|log)$"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId,
              DropPath = FolderPath, DropName = FileName, DropSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
// 3. Office spawns a LOLBin/script-host child (T1204.002 user-triggered)
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
| where FileName in~ (LoaderChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner Drops on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DropTime .. DropTime + 5m)
| join kind=inner PhishMail on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where DropTime between (MailTime .. MailTime + 1h)
| project MailTime, DropTime, ChildTime = Timestamp,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          SenderFromAddress, Subject, AttachmentName,
          ParentApp = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DropName, DropPath, DropSHA256,
          ChildBin = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by ChildTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let LoaderChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let ArchiveExt = dynamic([".zip",".rar",".cab",".7z",".iso",".img",".dat",".bin"]);
// 1. Office app drops compressed/encrypted secondary payload
let Drops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (OfficeParents)
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | where tolower(TargetFileName) endswith_any (ArchiveExt) or TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(dat|bin|tmp|log)$"
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              DropPath = TargetFilePath, DropName = TargetFileName,
              DropSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256, DropParent = ActingProcessName;
// 2. Office spawns LOLBin / script-host child within 5 min of the drop
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ParentProcessName has_any (OfficeParents)
| where TargetProcessName has_any (LoaderChildren)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner Drops on DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (DropTime .. DropTime + 5m)
| project DropTime, ChildTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentApp = ParentProcessName,
          DropName, DropPath, DropSHA256,
          ChildBin = TargetProcessName,
          ChildCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine
| order by ChildTime desc
ucs.rationale: Mofang's documented kill-chain (FOX-IT 2016) is spearphishing attachment -> Office macro / lure -> compressed-or-encrypted secondary payload (their ShimRat loader is XOR/RC4-encoded and packed) -> rundll32/regsvr32 execution. Most APT process-tree detections only check parent->child; this UC requires the full triad (delivered attachment + office-dropped archive/encoded blob + LOLBin spawn within 5

ucs.title: [LLM] Mofang lookalike-infrastructure click: spearphishing link to a domain impersonating the victim org
ucs.description: Hunts the Mofang trademark behaviour that gave the group its name — registering and operating C2/landing infrastructure that imitates the victim's own domain (homoglyph, typosquat, brand-substring). Catches T1566.002 spearphishing-link delivery + T1204.001 user click where the destination URL is structurally similar to one of the org's own domains but is NOT actually owned by the org.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.src) as src_hosts from datamodel=Web where Web.action="allowed" by Web.user Web.dest Web.http_referrer | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | rex field=dest "(?<reg_domain>[a-z0-9-]+\.[a-z]{2,}$)" | eval org_domains=split("contoso.com,contoso.gov,contoso.mil",",") | eval org_brands=split("contoso,cntoso,c0ntoso,contos0,c-ontoso",",") | eval brand_match=mvfilter(match(reg_domain, mvjoin(org_brands,"|"))) | where isnotnull(brand_match) AND NOT (reg_domain IN ("contoso.com","contoso.gov","contoso.mil")) | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.action="delivered" by All_Email.recipient All_Email.src_user All_Email.subject All_Email.url | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | rename recipient as user | rex field=url "https?:\/\/(?<reg_domain>[^\/]+)"] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime user reg_domain dest urls src_user subject src_hosts count
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
// CONFIGURE: list the org's real domains AND a generated set of homoglyph/typosquat substrings
let _org_domains = dynamic(["contoso.com","contoso.gov","contoso.mil"]);
let _org_brand_tokens = dynamic(["contoso","cntoso","c0ntoso","contos0","c-ontoso","contoso-corp","contoso-mail","contoso-gov"]);
// 1. Inbound emails carrying URLs whose registrable domain looks like ours but isn't ours
let LookalikeMail = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailUrlInfo
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | extend LowerDom = tolower(UrlDomain)
    | where LowerDom has_any (_org_brand_tokens)
    | where not(LowerDom endswith_any (_org_domains))
    | project MailTime = Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, RecipientEmailAddress,
              SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain, Subject,
              LookalikeUrl = Url, LookalikeDomain = LowerDom;
// 2. The user actually clicked through (T1204.001)
UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
| extend LowerClickDom = tolower(tostring(parse_url(Url).Host))
| where LowerClickDom has_any (_org_brand_tokens)
| where not(LowerClickDom endswith_any (_org_domains))
| join kind=inner LookalikeMail on $left.NetworkMessageId == $right.NetworkMessageId
| project ClickTime = Timestamp, MailTime, AccountUpn, IPAddress,
          SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain, Subject,
          LookalikeUrl, LookalikeDomain,
          IsClickedThrough, IsClickedThrough_signal = iif(ActionType == "ClickedThrough", "USER_BYPASSED_WARNING", "")
| order by ClickTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _org_domains = dynamic(["contoso.com","contoso.gov","contoso.mil"]);
let _org_brand_tokens = dynamic(["contoso","cntoso","c0ntoso","contos0","c-ontoso","contoso-corp","contoso-mail","contoso-gov"]);
// Sentinel side: OfficeActivity (Exchange) gives delivered-mail context;
// CommonSecurityLog / W3CIISLog gives proxy-side click telemetry.
let LookalikeMail = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Receive","Deliver")
    | extend LowerSubj = tolower(Subject)
    | where LowerSubj has_any (_org_brand_tokens)
    | project MailTime = TimeGenerated, MailboxOwnerUPN, Sender, Subject;
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where DeviceVendor in~ ("Zscaler","Palo Alto Networks","Cisco","Forcepoint","Symantec","Netskope")
| where isnotempty(RequestURL)
| extend Host = tolower(tostring(parse_url(RequestURL).Host))
| where Host has_any (_org_brand_tokens)
| where not(Host endswith_any (_org_domains))
| join kind=inner LookalikeMail on $left.SourceUserName == $right.MailboxOwnerUPN
| where TimeGenerated between (MailTime .. MailTime + 24h)
| project ClickTime = TimeGenerated, MailTime, SourceUserName, SourceIP,
          Sender, Subject, RequestURL, Host, DeviceAction, DeviceVendor
| order by ClickTime desc
ucs.rationale: Mofang's defining tradecraft (FOX-IT 2016: 'Mofang: A politically motivated information stealing adversary') is impersonating victim infrastructure — they register lookalike domains for the target's gov/military portals and stage second-stage downloads from them, which is why the cluster is named Mofang ('imitator' in Mandarin). A generic phishing-link rule fires on every malicious URL; this rule
